entry.title: Washington Post: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Russian-backed separatist court sentences three foreign fighters to death
  • Russian-backed separatist court hands death sentences to two Britons and a Moroccan who fought for Ukraine
  • Iran will remove 27 cameras from nuclear sites, U.N. watchdog says
  • Thailand legalizes marijuana — with gray areas and caveats
  • Belgian king offers ‘regrets’ but no apology for colonialism in Congo
  • A deadly disease is wiping out Caribbean coral, researchers find
  • WHO covid origins report says ‘lab leak’ theory needs further investigation
  • Microplastics found in fresh Antarctic snow, as pollutants spread
  • Swirling doubts herald major shifts at upcoming Chinese political meeting
  • Who are the Gupta brothers, the family accused of looting South Africa?
  • Hacked Russian radio station broadcasts Ukrainian anthem
  • U.N. seeks deal on food exports as war drives up prices and hurts millions worldwide
  • Ukraine, coping with war losses, adds new polish to military funerals
  • Why a 1970s-style ‘stagflation’ crisis would hit the world’s poor hard
  • Car crashes into Berlin crowd, killing 1; driver is detained
  • Top general details plan to train Ukrainians on rocket artillery
  • Migrants split on whether to keep walking through Mexico
  • UN elects new council members including Japan, Switzerland
  • Growing public pressure around Amazon disappearances
  • Spain evacuates 2,000 as crews race to counter wildfire
  • Activists decry French retailer over Amazon deforestation
  • Prosecutor: Driver in fatal Berlin crash seems mentally ill
  • Lightning storms in Albania flood cities, cause 1 death
  • Pakistanis rally to denounce India over remarks about Islam
  • Iran pulls UN nuke cameras in possible 'fatal blow' to deal
  • Officer who killed Patrick Lyoya charged with second-degree murder
  • Five Marines killed in Osprey crash in California desert
  • She got an STD during car sex. Now, Geico could pay her $5.2 million.
  • Teen beaten to death outside school funded by LeBron James foundation
  • Parents of trans children sue Texas to stop child-abuse investigations
  • Social agitators, joyfully Black: The artistic heirs of Gordon Parks
  • Police say brothers — 11 and 14 — broke into gun store, stole 22 weapons
  • Michigan gubernatorial candidate arrested on Jan. 6 Capitol riot charge
  • Post Politics Now: Jan. 6 panel to hear from Capitol Police officer, filmmaker as high-drama hearings begin
  • D.C. chiropractor being sued in officer’s assault is charged in Jan. 6 riot
  • Explosion at Texas LNG plant puts added strain on global energy market
  • Jewelry giant settles gender-discrimination lawsuit for $175 million
  • Mortgage rates move higher ahead of Federal Reserve meeting
  • Most Americans expect inflation to get worse, Post-Schar School poll finds
  • My co-worker is asking to borrow my clothes
  • Live updates: Summer Game Fest 2022 shows off ‘Modern Warfare 2,’ ‘Stormgate’ and ‘Cuphead’ DLC
  • NASA Inspector General issues a scathing report on moon effort
  • NASA joins the hunt for UFOs
  • Federal investigators step up probe into Tesla Autopilot crashes
  • Maxed-out townhouse sets the stage for minimal impact on environment
  • Why foundation cracks in your home shouldn’t be ignored
  • The Clock Is Ticking for Credit Suisse’s CEO
  • The Supreme Court Has a Nasty Surprise in Store for Business
  • ECB Delay Presages Bigger Rate Increases in Coming Months
  • Don’t Be Fooled by Conflicting Inflation Metrics. The Fed Won’t.
  • How Old-Style Buy Now, Pay Later Became Trendy ‘BNPL’
  • How Legal Weed Has Changed the US for Better and Worse
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • How Putin’s Spooking Japan Further Away From Pacifism
  • As our entitlements crisis gets closer, a solution has moved farther away
  • Americans deserve to know if ATF will continue ‘stash house stings’
  • How did the pandemic begin? China must help find the answer.
  • DeSantis attacks the Rays — and free speech
  • Tune out the ‘it won’t matter’ coverage of the Jan. 6 hearings
  • Conservatives, your radical legal revolution will not go unchallenged
  • How NIMBYism chokes off affordable housing even in Big Sky Country
  • My attacker has been jailed. But who was pulling the strings?
  • How Canada’s government is moving ahead on gun reform — while the U.S. stalls
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Trump and Unreleased Video Expected to Be Focus of First Jan 6. Hearing
  • Putin Compares Himself to Peter the Great, the Famous Czar
  • Russia Prosecuting 1,100 Captured Ukrainian Soldiers
  • Michigan Police Officer Charged With Murder After Killing Black Motorist
  • The New Abortion Bans: Almost No Exceptions for Rape, Incest or Health
  • Seeking a Return to Offices, Bosses Lost Leverage
  • German Brewers Face ‘Unprecedented’ Beer Bottle Shortage
  • Logan Ury Says You’re Dating All Wrong
  • Saudi Arabia’s LIV Golf Series Upends Genteel World of Golf
  • PGA Tour Suspends Players Who Joined Saudi-Backed LIV Golf Series
  • The Insurrection Didn’t End on Jan. 6. The Hearings Need to Prove That.

  • The Jan. 6 Committee Has Already Blown It
  • Liz Cheney Will Not Tolerate Trump’s Lies
  • David Axelrod, Sarah Longwell and Preet Bharara on What to Look For in the Jan. 6 Hearings
  • With Elon Musk, the Drama Is the Point
  • Russia and America Are Parting Ways
  • We Need Hope to Combat Violence. That Won’t Arrive Without Action.

  • The Proud Boys’ Path to Jan. 6
  • Modern Love Podcast: Marriage Classes at Guantánamo
  • The Racist Researcher Cited in the Buffalo Gunman’s Manifesto

  • Live Updates: At Americas Summit, Biden Calls for Fighting Poverty, Promoting Regional Progress

  • Naftali Bennett, Israel’s Prime Minister, Visits U.A.E.
  • At Least 7 Killed in Explosion and Fire in Daegu, South Korea
  • Russia Has Filled Ukrainian Water Canal to Crimea
  • Car Plows Into a School Group in Berlin, Killing 1 and Injuring 14

  • Dmitry Kovtun, Accused Killer of Russian Dissident, Dies at 56
  • U.S. Accelerated Expulsions of Haitian Migrants in May

  • Tourist Damages Rome’s Spanish Steps by Pushing a Scooter Down Them
  • 2 Officials in Vietnam Are Arrested in Covid Test Kit Scandal
  • A Radical Collective Takes Over One of the World’s Biggest Art Shows
  • White House Outlines Covid Vaccine Plan for Children Under 5
  • Biden Administration to Set Rules of the Road for Charging Electric Vehicles
  • Your Thursday Briefing
  • Your Thursday Briefing: Russia’s Land Bridge
  • Russia Is Racing to Cement Its Control in Southern Ukraine
  • With Lifeguards Scarce, It’s Beach Season That Needs Rescuing
  • Harvard’s President Will Step Down Next Year
  • A Vanishing Word in Abortion Debate: ‘Women’
  • In Uvalde, a Search for Answers: How Could This Happen?
  • Michigan Governor Candidate Ryan Kelley, Who Was at the Capitol on Jan. 6, Is Arrested
  • Plastic Water Bottles to Be Phased Out at National Parks
  • Summit of the Americas in Los Angeles Features President Biden, Kamala Harris
  • At Summit of Americas, Biden Pledges U.S. Help on Latin American Problems
  • As Survivors of Gun Violence Demand Action, House Passes Bill Doomed in the Senate
  • Five Marines Dead in Military Plane Crash in California
  • How South Dakota Voters Won a Power Struggle With G.O.P. Legislators
  • Brookings Puts Retired Gen. John Allen on Leave Amid Lobbying Inquiry
  • Jan. 6 Inquiry Votes Aren’t Costing G.O.P. Incumbents in Primaries, Yet
  • Oris Buckner, Detective Who Blew Whistle on Police Abuse, Dies at 70
  • House Democrats Dared Republicans to Oppose White Supremacy Measure. They Obliged.
  • Auto Safety Agency Expands Tesla Investigation
  • Disney Fires Peter Rice, Its Top TV Content Executive
  • European Central Bank says it will raise interest rates for the first time in 11 years in July.
  • The Billionaires Behind a Push to Reinvigorate U.S. Chip-Making
  • Warner Bros. Discovery Picks Sports Chief to Navigate Streaming Era
  • Billionaires, Biden and Chips
  • The Grammys Have a New Award: Songwriter of the Year
  • Jump in Mortgage Rates Could Add $100,000 to Housing Costs
  • ECB Prepares for First Interest Rate Increase in a Decade
  • Lawrence D. Ackman, a Cityscape’s Financier, Dies at 83
  • What Texas Gains by Investigating Twitter’s Fake Accounts
  • It’s Easier Than Ever to Travel Abroad Without Cash
  • Higher Inflation Predicted in Latest O.E.C.D. Outlook
  • Is It Finally Twilight for the Theater’s Sacred Monsters?
  • Twitter Said to Agree to Give Elon Musk Access to Stream of Tweets
  • You Won’t Use That Cool Feature
  • Dave Smith, Whose Synthesizers Shaped Electronic Music, Dies at 72

entry.title: Guardian: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Switzerland v Spain, Portugal v Czech Republic: Nations League – live!
  • US braces for House committee’s primetime January 6 hearings – live
  • Russia-Ukraine war latest: fierce fighting in Sievierodonetsk; UK ‘deeply concerned’ by death sentences for Britons in Donetsk – live
  • Most senior Uefa security officials ‘not actively involved’ in Champions League final control room
  • Brady won’t rule out another no-confidence vote on Johnson within a year
  • Premier League to issue football-wide bans for pitch invaders next season
  • The Guardian view on overriding the Brexit treaty: a national disgrace | Editorial
  • Removal of cameras at Iran nuclear sites ‘fatal blow’ to talks, warns UN watchdog
  • Everyone is sick - illness in 2022 has medieval vibes | Brigid Delaney
  • Getting over the hassle of going out: ‘Select one or two things to start with’
  • Who are the Britons condemned by Russian ‘show trial’ in Ukraine?
  • US justice department to investigate Louisiana State Police following 2019 death of Ronald Greene
  • Panel to connect Proud Boys and Oath Keepers in Capitol attack conspiracy
  • Ben Stokes promises England will be ‘even more positive’ in second Test
  • ‘I won’t believe humans any more’: Johnny Depp releases self-penned ballad lamenting fame
  • Islamic State affiliate suspected of Catholic church massacre, Nigeria says

  • Sue Barker announces that this year’s Wimbledon will be her last as presenter
  • Washington Commanders coach sorry after calling Capitol attack a ‘dust-up’
  • Britons sentenced to death after ‘show trial’ in Russian-occupied Ukraine
  • Dom Phillips: sister of missing journalist still hopeful he will be found
  • Irish exorcist calls for extra help for people oppressed by evil spirits
  • Pressure mounts on Senate to act on gun safety amid Republican resistance
  • The west’s calls for a total victory in Ukraine can lead only to ruinous escalation | Simon Jenkins
  • Scientists make ‘slightly sweaty’ robot finger with living skin
  • Huge mystery spill detected in Baltic off Swedish coast
  • What to know about the public January 6 committee hearings
  • ‘I was losing words. I didn’t remember the week before’: Beth Orton on chronic illness, MeToo and motherhood
  • Why the televised hearings on the January 6 insurrection will be historic | Robert Reich
  • Shakespeare First Folio copy estimated to fetch $2.5m in New York auction
  • Javari valley: the lawless primal wilderness where Dom Phillips went missing
  • Steve Reich: Reich/Richter review | Andrew Clements's classical album of the week
  • Unseen works by ‘queen of gothic fiction’ Shirley Jackson published
  • PGA Tour suspends all golfers competing at Saudi-backed LIV event
  • ‘Narcissistic’ UK politics putting EU relations at risk, says former British envoy
  • Anyika Onuora: ‘I couldn’t tell anyone – a lot of abuse in sport is swept under the rug’
  • ‘All hell broke loose’: weary soldiers tell of frontline holdout in Ukraine city
  • Fleetwood Mac’s Christine McVie: ‘Cocaine and champagne made me perform better’
  • European Central Bank to raise interest rates for first time since 2011
  • Bones found on Isle of Wight may be from Europe’s biggest predator dinosaur
  • Cursed Child star Noma Dumezweni: ‘As a Black woman in acting, I’m fearless now’
  • R Kelly should get at least 25 years in prison, prosecutors say
  • The disappearance of journalist Dom Phillips in Brazil should leave you incandescent with rage | Lucy Jordan
  • Li Jiaqi: Chinese influencer’s career hangs in balance after ‘tank cake’ stream
  • Boston kept faith when hard times hit. Now they’re two wins from a title
  • Judy Garland’s 20 best films – ranked!
  • Dining across the divide: ‘I was expecting a vegan wearing a European Union hat - one of those’
  • Neneh Cherry: The Versions review | Alexis Petridis's album of the week
  • How Messy Millennial Woman became TV’s most tedious trope
  • Climate policy dragged into culture wars as a ‘delay’ tactic, finds study
  • Record feats, lawsuit grief and naming retired popes – take the Thursday quiz
  • ‘So gorgeous you’ll shed tears of joy’: readers’ favourite beaches in Greece
  • ‘It was the best day of my life!’ Guardian readers on Abba Voyage
  • Since George Floyd’s death sport, not politics, has led the fight against racism | Mihir Bose
  • Huge scale and impact of Israeli incursions over Lebanon skies revealed
  • The mayor thinks New York gets ‘special energy’ from crystals. Is he right?
  • I took the train to Strasbourg, France – here’s my guide to the city
  • Shanghai to lock down 2.7 million, a week after easing Covid restrictions
  • EU regulators ‘dismissed evidence’ linking glyphosate to rodent tumours
  • ‘It’s a war on the people’: El Salvador’s mass arrests send thousands into despair
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 9 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-09 18:27PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Former employee of scandal-hit Hong Kong child protection group pleads guilty to abuse, first of 27 suspects to do so
  • Hong Kong bosses should be flexible with workers caring for elderly or disabled residents at home, study says
  • Owner of domestic helper agency in Hong Kong denies wrongdoing over death of worker
  • Hong Kong heritage advisers urge officials to work with advocates to reassess value of pre-war building once used for spying against Japanese during World War II
  • 8 Labrador puppies bred to detect drugs, explosives under programme between customs, Hong Kong airport’s security company
  • New training school for Hong Kong government workers to focus on raising awareness of national security, college head says
  • Hong Kong police arrest 46 in crackdown on syndicate using modem-pool devices in new tactic to harass debtors into paying up
  • Japan reopens to visitors on June 10, but Hong Kong travellers may have to delay departure, with visa applications up in the air
  • Credit card company sues wife of former Hong Kong leader CY Leung for failing to pay HK$90,000 bill
  • Hong Kong teenagers arrested, 10.5kg of ketamine seized during crackdown on drug syndicate
  • Hong Kong leader rules out reopening border with mainland China any time soon, pointing to nation’s tough Covid-19 policy
  • Hong Kong invites global financiers to two-day November summit in much-heralded bid to reclaim city’s spot in world finance
  • 2 jobless Hong Kong men jailed 10 years for causing woman’s death in snatch theft, judge calls crime a ‘cowardly attack’
  • Body of missing Hong Kong hiker, 52, found a day after she was swept away while swimming in country park stream
  • Hong Kong passes long-awaited labour bill to scrap MPF offsetting mechanism, protecting workers’ pensions
  • Early years matters
  • As it happened: ‘No chance of border with mainland China reopening in near future’, Hong Kong’s Carrie Lam says in last Legco session
  • Hong Kong set to double levy on plastic bags, charge producers and suppliers extra for glass beverage bottles in drive to reduce waste
  • Coronavirus: Aji Ichiban co-founder may lower rents for vacant shops after Covid-19 losses forced once-thriving Hong Kong snack chain to shut down
  • Look beyond dollar signs to see Jumbo Floating Restaurant’s true value to Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong’s Fire Services Department readies 2,000 additional officers to handle possible 6th Covid-19 wave, in bid to ease strain on paramedics
  • Hong Kong universities’ international reputation takes a hit in latest world rankings, but result still ‘broadly positive’
  • In the eyes of others, the US is not the benign power it thinks it is
  • WHO says Covid-19 origins unclear but lab leak theory needs study
  • China-Germany economic ties shift with Xinjiang allegations, zero Covid and supply chain issues
  • Inflation isn’t China’s problem, income is: ‘all we can do is spend less’
  • China’s chip import volume declines in first five months as rigid Covid-19 control measures disrupt manufacturing sector
  • Beijing hits out at Washington over proposed US$120 million arms deal to Taiwan
  • Taiwan is installing Bee Eye radars on its outlying islands to track PLA buzzing
  • Ant Group, China’s securities regulator deny the fintech giant is reviving stalled IPO
  • European Parliament passes landslide vote on China’s alleged rights abuses in Xinjiang
  • Chinese state broadcasting gets a new chief: Xi protégé Xu Lin
  • Chinese court awards woman US$4,500 compensation for 7 years of housework and child-rearing from ex-husband after divorce
  • China digital currency: e-CNY ‘well positioned’ but still ‘a way to go’ to challenge US dollar dominance
  • Xiaohongshu, dubbed China’s Instagram, tests Web3 waters with investment in US start-up Shil.me
  • Xi Jinping tells officials to ensure economic and social stability ahead of Communist Party congress
  • Shanghai moves towards ‘watershed moment’ with Disney resort reopening partially; city reports six new Covid-19 cases
  • Alibaba, Meituan paid the bulk of China’s US$3 billion antitrust fines in 2021, report shows
  • Chinese military jet crash leaves 1 dead, 2 injured and houses on fire in central Hubei province
  • 10 dead, hundreds of thousands displaced as heavy rains lash Hunan, central China
  • Video: Large sinkhole discovered in southern China, the 30th of its kind in the same county
  • China trade: export growth in May boosted by Shanghai reopening, but ‘temporary blip’ to fade amid headwinds
  • Video: Chinese scientists develop robot worm to explore engine pipes
  • Chinese blockchain platform ChainMaker says it has new technology to keep it secure from quantum attacks
  • Man in China seeks divorce from wife of 16 years after paternity tests reveal 3 daughters he raised are not his children
  • ‘It’s tough’: lockdown drags on for millions in Dandong, near North Korea
  • Stinking piles of rubbish are damaging tourism and locals are ‘forced to live like pigs’ in Nepal’s capital city
  • Robert Kuok still tops Malaysia’s rich list with US$11 billion as wealth of country’s tycoons tumbles 10 per cent
  • China and Singapore to resume military exercises, cooperate on military education
  • Philippines billionaire Razon plans massive solar panel facility
  • Sri Lanka president’s brother, Basil Rajapaksa, resigns from parliament
  • How BJP’s Nupur Sharma’s Prophet Mohammed comments pushed India into diplomatic maelstrom
  • Two Chinese men arrested over alleged plot to cheat on Japan university exam
  • North Korea’s record missile flurry could have cost as much as US$650 million
  • Coronavirus: Japan has dropped its outdoor mask mandate, but the message has not filtered through to the public
  • South Korea office building fire kills at least 7, arson suspected
  • Calls grow to boycott Indian goods in Mideast following insults of Prophet Muhammad and Islam by members of Narendra Modi’s BJP
  • Bon Odori Festival: Malaysian royal intervention saves Japan dance festival from Muslim critics
  • Indonesian navy officers asked for US$375,000 to release tanker off its waters near Singapore
  • Japan’s June 10 reopening: tourism sector braces for surge of travel-starved visitors eager to ‘experience, not see’ the country
  • US confrontation or Chinese cooperation? As the Asia-Pacific contemplates its future security, the choice is obvious
  • Hailing Peter the Great, Vladimir Putin draws parallel with Ukraine mission to ‘return’ Russian lands
  • Covid during pregnancy doubled babies’ risk of delayed development in speech, motor skills
  • Convicted R&B singer R Kelly should get more than 25 years in prison, say prosecutors
  • Ukraine: Death penalty issued to captured British, Moroccan fighters by Russian forces in Donbas
  • Battle for Sievierodonetsk ‘brutal’, Zelenksy says as Russia pushes Ukrainian troops to city’s outskirts
  • Russia attacking Ukraine food targets to scare world: governor
  • Russia cracks down on critics of its ‘military operation’ in Ukraine
  • New US nuclear-missile submarines hobbled by billions in growing costs and delays
  • WHO warns of ‘real’ risk as monkeypox outbreak tops 1,000 cases
  • Iran switches off nuclear site cameras as IAEA censure adopted
  • Belgium’s king expresses regret, but offers no apology for colonial past in landmark Congo trip
  • US Olympians and dozens of other women sue FBI over botched Larry Nassar sex abuse probe
  • Three US companies sanctioned for sending defence blueprints to China
  • With Xinjiang top of mind, EU lawmakers urge more hardline approach on China human rights
  • Girl, 11, tells US Congress how she covered herself in blood to survive Texas school shooting
  • Electric cars: Nio warns of fewer deliveries this quarter as anti-pandemic curbs hit production, weaken sales
  • Alibaba’s cloud services unit jumps on NFT bandwagon amid a global market downturn for these digital assets
  • UBS expects family offices’ investments in China to cool down amid regulatory crackdown, real estate stress
  • Buffett-backed BYD boosts price ceiling by 33 per cent for stock buy-back plan in vote of confidence on market outlook
  • Why the yuan isn’t a big winner in shift away from US dollar
  • New World Development’s Humansa expands footprint in health and wellness in Greater Bay Area with acquisition and partnership
  • Ant Group’s latest Singapore expansion brings mobile payment apps from Malaysia and South Korea to the city
  • Apple to handle lending itself for new Pay Later service, but keeps partnership with Goldman Sachs for credit card
  • Hang Seng slides as Meituan, JD.com and tech leaders limp while CNOOC advances on oil price outlook
  • Sunac, China’s fourth-largest developer by sales, to delay payment of US$345 million onshore bond due on Monday, sources say
  • Twitter will give Elon Musk ‘firehose’ of raw tweet data in dispute over bots, reports say
  • Alibaba’s cloud services business launches two new data centres in Saudi Arabia to step up its overseas expansion
  • Disney+ K-drama Link: Eat, Love, Kill – Yeo Jin-goo, Moon Ga-young make a compelling pair in engaging fantasy comedy-drama-mystery
  • New art fair Tokyo Gendai will introduce international contemporary artists to a younger generation of Japanese collectors
  • When Thai food meets Sichuan: Hong Kong chef talks spices, mixing the two cuisines and TCM food at new restaurant Sawadee Chef
  • It takes a hardy tourist to visit Sri Lanka amid protests and power cuts
  • How AI, data are transforming healthcare: three key trends that could change the face of medicine
  • Kristen Stewart and fiancée Dylan Meyer are one of Hollywood’s most stylish couples
  • Whatever happened to Kate Bush, singer of Stranger Things hit song Running Up That Hill? From her US$60 million net worth and reclusive lifestyle, to her 1985 hit making a chart comeback
  • Inside Johnny Depp’s OTT US$100 million property portfolio: from the Bahamas island where he married Amber Heard, to a French village he bought Vanessa Paradis and a Kentucky farm named after his mum
  • Who is Harry Styles’ girlfriend Olivia Wilde, really? The Tron actress based her stage name on Oscar Wilde, has two kids with Ted Lasso star Jason Sudeikis, and was once even married to a prince
  • Who are BTS’ celebrity pals in Hollywood? The K-pop idols hung with Coldplay’s Chris Martin after meeting Joe Biden at the White House, and are close with Lizzo, Halsey and Megan Thee Stallion too
  • The rise and fall of Hong Kong’s Jumbo Floating Restaurant: celebrities from Queen Elizabeth to Tom Cruise flocked to the iconic tourist destination – but what will happen to it now?
  • On Her Watch: Why high jewellery and Métiers d’Art timepieces are the ultimate luxury for women, from Chanel’s Mademoiselle Privé collection to Bruguet and Jaquet Droz watches
  • The extravagant lives of India’s disgraced Gupta family: from mansions in Dubai and South Africa, and Lamborghini and Porsche supercars, to ‘big fat Gupta’ weddings, Atul and Rajesh splashed millions
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

  • VOX POPULI: Working solo at an eatery is like climbing a mountain alone
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
